methods: MELODYFLOW: a single-stage text-conditioned Flow Matching (FM) model for music generation and editing
Diffusion Transformer architecture trained on a flow-matching objective
Regularized FM latent inversion method adapted from RENOISE for improved editability
Comparison with original RENOISE and DDIM inversion methods for zero-shot test-time editing
Ablation study on model design choices
problems: High-fidelity, text-guided music editing across diverse tasks (e.g., inpainting, outpainting, looping, instrument/genre swapping, vocals removal, lyrics editing, tempo control, recording condition modification)
Lack of generic, high-fidelity style transfer methods for music editing
Need for efficient, fast inference in high-fidelity (48 kHz stereo) music editing
Insufficient generalization and editability in existing music editing models
datasets: Not explicitly named in the provided content
metrics: Objective metrics for music editing (not specified in the excerpt)

results.value: Regularized latent inversion outperforms both RENOISE and DDIM for zero-shot test-time text-guided editing
Noticeable improvement over previous state of the art for music editing

novelty_claims: First single-stage text-to-music Flow Matching (FM) model for generating and editing 48 kHz stereo samples up to 30 seconds
Novel regularized FM inversion method for faithful zero-shot test-time text-guided editing while maintaining coherence with the original sample
Enhanced audio latent representation and generative model for improved quality/efficiency trade-off
Demonstrated support for diverse editing tasks on real songs without finetuning, with fast editing, high consistency, text-adherence, and minimal quality loss
Public release of code and model weights to foster research in music editing

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Rectified flow matching for video-to-audio generation","Non-autoregressive vector field estimator using a feed-forward transformer","Channel-level cross-modal feature fusion for strong temporal alignment","Reflow and one-step distillation with guided vector field for efficient sampling"],"problems":["Generating high-quality audio from silent video (video-to-audio generation)","Achieving strong temporal alignment between generated audio and input video","Improving generation efficiency (speed and resource utilization) in V2A models"],"datasets":["VGGSound"],"metrics":["Inception Score (IS)","Temporal alignment accuracy","Generation speed (relative to baseline)"],"results":[{"metric":"Temporal alignment accuracy (25 steps)","value":"97.22%"},{"metric":"Inception Score (improvement over baseline)","value":"6.2% improvement over diffusion-based baseline"},{"metric":"Generation speed (relative to Diff-Foley)","value":"7.3x faster"},{"metric":"Temporal alignment accuracy (1 step, with reflow and distillation)","value":"97.85%"},{"metric":"Generation speed (1 step vs 25 steps)","value":"9.3x acceleration"}],"novelty_claims":["First application of rectified flow matching to video-to-audio generation","Non-autoregressive vector field estimator with feed-forward transformer preserves temporal resolution for better alignment","Channel-level cross-modal feature fusion for strong audio-visual alignment","Integration of reflow and one-step distillation enables high-quality audio generation in very few (even one) sampling steps, greatly improving efficiency","Achieves state-of-the-art performance in both audio quality and temporal alignment on VGGSound"]}

methods: Rectified flow matching for video-to-audio generation
Non-autoregressive vector field estimator using a feed-forward transformer
Channel-level cross-modal feature fusion for strong temporal alignment
Reflow and one-step distillation with guided vector field for efficient sampling
problems: Generating high-quality audio from silent video (video-to-audio generation)
Achieving strong temporal alignment between generated audio and input video
Improving generation efficiency (speed and resource utilization) in V2A models

results.value: 6.2% improvement over diffusion-based baseline
7.3x faster

results.metric: Temporal alignment accuracy (1 step, with reflow and distillation)

results.value: 9.3x acceleration

novelty_claims: First application of rectified flow matching to video-to-audio generation
Non-autoregressive vector field estimator with feed-forward transformer preserves temporal resolution for better alignment
Channel-level cross-modal feature fusion for strong audio-visual alignment
Integration of reflow and one-step distillation enables high-quality audio generation in very few (even one) sampling steps, greatly improving efficiency
Achieves state-of-the-art performance in both audio quality and temporal alignment on VGGSound

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["JEN-1: a universal text-to-music generation model based on diffusion models","Hybrid autoregressive (AR) and non-autoregressive (NAR) training in an end-to-end manner","Masked noise-robust autoencoder for continuous embedding representation and latent embedding normalization","Direct modeling of raw waveform data (up to 48kHz stereo)","Multi-task in-context learning for text-guided music generation, music inpainting, and continuation"],"problems":["Generating high-fidelity, realistic music from textual descriptions (text-to-music)","Overcoming limitations of existing models in music quality, computational efficiency, and generalization","Avoiding fidelity loss from spectrogram-based approaches","Unifying multiple music generation and editing tasks in a single model"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Objective metrics (not specified in detail)","Human evaluation (perceptual quality, text-music alignment)"],"results":[{"metric":"Human evaluation (perceptual quality)","value":"JEN-1 produces music of perceptually higher quality compared to state-of-the-art methods (e.g., 85.7 vs. 83.8)"},{"metric":"Text-music alignment (human judgment)","value":"JEN-1 generates music highly aligned with text prompts"}],"novelty_claims":["First model to unify text-to-music, music inpainting, and music continuation in a single non-cascade model with both AR and NAR diffusion modes","Direct modeling of high-fidelity (48kHz stereo) raw waveforms without spectrogram conversion loss","Masked noise-robust autoencoder with continuous embedding and latent normalization for high-quality music generation","Superior performance in both objective and human evaluations over state-of-the-art methods while maintaining computational efficiency"]}

methods: JEN-1: a universal text-to-music generation model based on diffusion models
Hybrid autoregressive (AR) and non-autoregressive (NAR) training in an end-to-end manner
Masked noise-robust autoencoder for continuous embedding representation and latent embedding normalization
Direct modeling of raw waveform data (up to 48kHz stereo)
Multi-task in-context learning for text-guided music generation, music inpainting, and continuation
problems: Generating high-fidelity, realistic music from textual descriptions (text-to-music)
Overcoming limitations of existing models in music quality, computational efficiency, and generalization
Avoiding fidelity loss from spectrogram-based approaches
Unifying multiple music generation and editing tasks in a single model

results.value: JEN-1 produces music of perceptually higher quality compared to state-of-the-art methods (e.g., 85.7 vs. 83.8)
JEN-1 generates music highly aligned with text prompts

novelty_claims: First model to unify text-to-music, music inpainting, and music continuation in a single non-cascade model with both AR and NAR diffusion modes
Direct modeling of high-fidelity (48kHz stereo) raw waveforms without spectrogram conversion loss
Masked noise-robust autoencoder with continuous embedding and latent normalization for high-quality music generation
Superior performance in both objective and human evaluations over state-of-the-art methods while maintaining computational efficiency

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["AudioLDM: a text-to-audio generation system based on latent diffusion models (LDMs) in a continuous latent space","Use of mel-spectrogram-based variational auto-encoder (VAE) to encode audio into latent space","Conditioning LDMs on contrastive language-audio pretraining (CLAP) embeddings for both audio and text","Training LDMs with audio-only data, removing the need for paired audio-text data","Zero-shot text-guided audio manipulations (e.g., style transfer, inpainting, super-resolution) via the reverse diffusion process"],"problems":["Improving the quality and efficiency of text-to-audio (TTA) generation systems","Reducing the reliance on large-scale, high-quality audio-text paired datasets for TTA","Enabling flexible, zero-shot text-guided audio manipulations (such as style transfer, inpainting)"],"datasets":["AudioCaps"],"metrics":["Frechet Distance (FD)","Subjective evaluation metrics (not specified in detail)"],"results":[{"metric":"Frechet Distance (FD) on AudioCaps","value":"23.31"},{"metric":"Frechet Distance (FD) of DiffSound baseline","value":"47.68"}],"novelty_claims":["First attempt to develop a continuous latent diffusion model (LDM) for text-to-audio generation","AudioLDM outperforms existing methods in both subjective and objective metrics","Utilization of CLAP embeddings enables TTA generation without using language-audio pairs to train LDMs","Demonstration that training LDMs with audio-only data can achieve high-quality and computationally efficient TTA","First TTA system to enable zero-shot text-guided audio manipulations (style transfer, super-resolution, inpainting) without task-specific finetuning"]}

methods: AudioLDM: a text-to-audio generation system based on latent diffusion models (LDMs) in a continuous latent space
Use of mel-spectrogram-based variational auto-encoder (VAE) to encode audio into latent space
Conditioning LDMs on contrastive language-audio pretraining (CLAP) embeddings for both audio and text
Training LDMs with audio-only data, removing the need for paired audio-text data
Zero-shot text-guided audio manipulations (e.g., style transfer, inpainting, super-resolution) via the reverse diffusion process
problems: Improving the quality and efficiency of text-to-audio (TTA) generation systems
Reducing the reliance on large-scale, high-quality audio-text paired datasets for TTA
Enabling flexible, zero-shot text-guided audio manipulations (such as style transfer, inpainting)
metrics: Subjective evaluation metrics (not specified in detail)
novelty_claims: First attempt to develop a continuous latent diffusion model (LDM) for text-to-audio generation
AudioLDM outperforms existing methods in both subjective and objective metrics
Utilization of CLAP embeddings enables TTA generation without using language-audio pairs to train LDMs
Demonstration that training LDMs with audio-only data can achieve high-quality and computationally efficient TTA
First TTA system to enable zero-shot text-guided audio manipulations (style transfer, super-resolution, inpainting) without task-specific finetuning

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ZEro-shot Text-based Audio (ZETA) editing: zero-shot text-guided audio editing using DDPM inversion with pre-trained diffusion models, adapted from the image domain","ZEro-shot UnSupervized (ZEUS) editing: zero-shot unsupervised audio editing using DDPM inversion, discovering semantically meaningful editing directions via principal component analysis of the denoiser's posterior","Edit-friendly DDPM inversion: extracting latent noise vectors corresponding to the source signal for editing"],"problems":["Zero-shot audio editing using pre-trained diffusion models","Text-based and unsupervised editing of audio signals without retraining or test-time optimization","Generating semantically meaningful and musically plausible modifications to audio signals while preserving structure"],"datasets":["Not explicitly specified in the provided content"],"metrics":["Perceptual quality","Semantic similarity to the source signal"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of ZEUS, a novel zero-shot unsupervised audio editing method that discovers semantically meaningful editing directions without supervision","First adaptation of zero-shot text-based editing (ZETA) from the image domain to the audio domain using DDPM inversion","Demonstration that both ZETA and ZEUS enable a wide range of musically meaningful edits (e.g., changing instruments, genre, or melody) while maintaining high perceptual quality and semantic similarity","Outperforming existing zero-shot audio editing methods (SDEdit, DDIM inversion with AudioLDM2, and MusicGen) in generating semantically meaningful modifications faithful to the original signal"]}

methods: ZEro-shot Text-based Audio (ZETA) editing: zero-shot text-guided audio editing using DDPM inversion with pre-trained diffusion models, adapted from the image domain

ZEro-shot UnSupervized (ZEUS) editing: zero-shot unsupervised audio editing using DDPM inversion, discovering semantically meaningful editing directions via principal component analysis of the denoiser's posterior
Edit-friendly DDPM inversion: extracting latent noise vectors corresponding to the source signal for editing
problems: Zero-shot audio editing using pre-trained diffusion models
Text-based and unsupervised editing of audio signals without retraining or test-time optimization
Generating semantically meaningful and musically plausible modifications to audio signals while preserving structure
datasets: Not explicitly specified in the provided content
metrics: Semantic similarity to the source signal
novelty_claims: Introduction of ZEUS, a novel zero-shot unsupervised audio editing method that discovers semantically meaningful editing directions without supervision
First adaptation of zero-shot text-based editing (ZETA) from the image domain to the audio domain using DDPM inversion

Demonstration that both ZETA and ZEUS enable a wide range of musically meaningful edits (e.g., changing instruments, genre, or melody) while maintaining high perceptual quality and semantic similarity
Outperforming existing zero-shot audio editing methods (SDEdit, DDIM inversion with AudioLDM2, and MusicGen) in generating semantically meaningful modifications faithful to the original signal

methods: Latent diffusion model for audio generation (Stable Audio)
Fully-convolutional variational autoencoder for latent representation
Conditioning on text prompts and timing embeddings for content and length control
Fréchet Distance based on OpenL3 embeddings for evaluation

CLAP score for text-audio alignment evaluation
problems: Efficient generation of long-form, variable-length stereo music and sound effects at 44.1kHz from text prompts
Controlling the duration and structure of generated audio

novelty_claims: First to enable efficient, fast generation of long-form, variable-length stereo music and sound effects at 44.1kHz from text prompts using latent diffusion.
Introduction of timing embeddings for fine control over both content and length of generated audio.
Proposed new evaluation metrics for long-form, full-band stereo audio: OpenL3-based Fréchet Distance, KL divergence for semantic correspondence, and CLAP score for text-audio alignment.
Demonstrated ability to generate structured music (with intro, development, outro) and stereo sound effects, unlike previous models.
Achieves state-of-the-art results on long-form full-band stereo music and sound effects generation from text and timing inputs.

methods: Cascading diffusion models for music generation (generator and cascader models)
Intermediate representation using either log-mel spectrogram or low-fidelity audio waveform
Conditioning diffusion models on text prompts via pre-trained language model embeddings and cross-attention

Demonstrate the ability to generate music that reflects both coarse and fine-grained semantics from text prompts, beyond simple attribute conditioning.

methods: Mustango: a music-domain-knowledge-inspired text-to-music diffusion model
MuNet: a Music-Domain-Knowledge-Informed UNet guidance module for conditioning on chords, beats, tempo, and key

problems: Lack of controllability in text-to-music generation models, especially for musical attributes like chords, beats, tempo, and key

metrics: Controllability (ability to follow specified chords, beats, tempo, key)

~53,000 music-text pairs (11x larger than MusicCaps)

novelty_claims: First text-to-music diffusion model (Mustango) with explicit controllability over chords, beats, tempo, and key via MuNet guidance module.
Introduction of MusicBench, a large-scale dataset with musically-enriched captions and diversified music samples, enabling better training for controllable text-to-music generation.

Demonstrated state-of-the-art controllability and musicality in generated music compared to existing models.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["MusicLM: a hierarchical sequence-to-sequence model for conditional music generation from text","Multi-stage autoregressive modeling (leveraging AudioLM) with text conditioning","Use of MuLan joint music-text embedding model for conditioning and to address paired data scarcity","Extension to accept melody (audio) as additional conditioning signal (e.g., whistling, humming)"],"problems":["Generating high-fidelity, coherent music from text descriptions","Scarcity of paired audio-text data for training text-to-music models","Lack of evaluation datasets for text-to-music generation","Difficulty in capturing salient characteristics of music in text descriptions"],"datasets":["MusicCaps: 5.5k music-text pairs with expert-provided captions (publicly released)"],"metrics":["Audio quality (human evaluation)","Adherence to text description (human evaluation)","Memorization study (token sequence similarity)"],"results":[{"metric":"Audio quality (human evaluation)","value":"MusicLM outperforms previous systems such as Mubert and Riffusion"},{"metric":"Adherence to text description (human evaluation)","value":"MusicLM outperforms previous systems such as Mubert and Riffusion"},{"metric":"Memorization (token sequence similarity)","value":"Generated sequences significantly differ from training set sequences"}],"novelty_claims":["First model to generate high-fidelity, long, and coherent music (up to several minutes) from complex text descriptions at 24 kHz.","Introduction of a hierarchical sequence-to-sequence approach for text-to-music generation.","Use of MuLan embeddings to enable training on large audio-only corpora, addressing paired data scarcity.","Extension of text-to-music generation to accept melody as an additional conditioning signal (e.g., whistling, humming).","Release of MusicCaps, the first high-quality, hand-curated dataset for text-to-music generation."]}

methods: MusicLM: a hierarchical sequence-to-sequence model for conditional music generation from text

problems: Generating high-fidelity, coherent music from text descriptions

MusicLM outperforms previous systems such as Mubert and Riffusion
Generated sequences significantly differ from training set sequences

novelty_claims: First model to generate high-fidelity, long, and coherent music (up to several minutes) from complex text descriptions at 24 kHz.
Introduction of a hierarchical sequence-to-sequence approach for text-to-music generation.
Use of MuLan embeddings to enable training on large audio-only corpora, addressing paired data scarcity.
Extension of text-to-music generation to accept melody as an additional conditioning signal (e.g., whistling, humming).
Release of MusicCaps, the first high-quality, hand-curated dataset for text-to-music generation.

Integration of frame-level content-based controls for precise, steerable music editing (including arrangement, inpainting, and refinement).
Ability to perform flexible, long-term music editing (filling segments longer than 8 seconds), surpassing the short-range limitations of previous models.
First to enable high-quality, steerable music generation and editing via content-based controls in an autoregressive music audio language model.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Diffusion Inference-Time T-Optimization (DITTO): optimizing initial noise latents of pre-trained text-to-music diffusion models at inference-time using differentiable feature matching loss","Gradient checkpointing for memory efficiency during optimization"],"problems":["Controlling pre-trained text-to-music diffusion models at inference-time without supervised training or fine-tuning","Achieving flexible, high-quality, and training-free control over music generation tasks such as inpainting, outpainting, looping, intensity, melody, and musical structure control"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Controllability","Audio quality","Computational efficiency (time and memory usage)"],"results":[{"metric":"Controllability","value":"DITTO achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming MultiDiffusion, FreeDoM, Guidance Gradients, Music ControlNet, and DOODL"},{"metric":"Audio quality","value":"DITTO achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to baselines"},{"metric":"Computational efficiency (time)","value":"DITTO is 2x faster than comparable optimization-based methods"},{"metric":"Computational efficiency (memory)","value":"DITTO uses half the memory compared to comparable optimization-based methods"}],"novelty_claims":["DITTO is a novel, training-free framework for controlling pre-trained text-to-music diffusion models at inference-time by optimizing initial noise latents.","DITTO enables flexible, high-quality, and training-free control for a wide range of music generation tasks, including new tasks such as looping and musical structure control.","DITTO leverages gradient checkpointing for memory efficiency without compromising the sampling process.","DITTO achieves state-of-the-art controllability, audio quality, and computational efficiency compared to existing methods."]}

methods: Diffusion Inference-Time T-Optimization (DITTO): optimizing initial noise latents of pre-trained text-to-music diffusion models at inference-time using differentiable feature matching loss
Gradient checkpointing for memory efficiency during optimization
problems: Controlling pre-trained text-to-music diffusion models at inference-time without supervised training or fine-tuning
Achieving flexible, high-quality, and training-free control over music generation tasks such as inpainting, outpainting, looping, intensity, melody, and musical structure control

results.value: DITTO achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming MultiDiffusion, FreeDoM, Guidance Gradients, Music ControlNet, and DOODL
DITTO achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to baselines
DITTO is 2x faster than comparable optimization-based methods
DITTO uses half the memory compared to comparable optimization-based methods

novelty_claims: DITTO is a novel, training-free framework for controlling pre-trained text-to-music diffusion models at inference-time by optimizing initial noise latents.
DITTO enables flexible, high-quality, and training-free control for a wide range of music generation tasks, including new tasks such as looping and musical structure control.
DITTO leverages gradient checkpointing for memory efficiency without compromising the sampling process.
DITTO achieves state-of-the-art controllability, audio quality, and computational efficiency compared to existing methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Instruction-tuned LLM (FLAN-T5) as text encoder for text-to-audio generation","Latent Diffusion Model (LDM) for audio generation","Audio pressure level-based sound mixing for data augmentation","Keeping the text encoder frozen during LDM training"],"problems":["Improving text-to-audio (TTA) generation quality","Overcoming limitations of non-instruction-tuned or joint text-audio encoders in TTA","Effective data augmentation for audio mixing that preserves both source audios"],"datasets":["AudioCaps"],"metrics":["Objective metrics (not specified in excerpt)","Subjective metrics (not specified in excerpt)"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance on AudioCaps test set (objective and subjective metrics)","value":"TANGO outperforms AudioLDM on most metrics and is comparable on the rest, despite using 63x less training data"}],"novelty_claims":["First to use a frozen instruction-tuned LLM (FLAN-T5) as the text encoder for text-to-audio generation, instead of a joint text-audio encoder like CLAP or a non-instruction-tuned model.","Demonstrates that LDM can learn text-to-audio mapping from a much smaller dataset when guided by a strong instruction-tuned LLM, without fine-tuning the text encoder.","Introduces audio pressure level-based mixing for data augmentation, improving over random mixing used in prior works."]}

methods: Instruction-tuned LLM (FLAN-T5) as text encoder for text-to-audio generation
Latent Diffusion Model (LDM) for audio generation
Audio pressure level-based sound mixing for data augmentation
Keeping the text encoder frozen during LDM training
problems: Improving text-to-audio (TTA) generation quality
Overcoming limitations of non-instruction-tuned or joint text-audio encoders in TTA
Effective data augmentation for audio mixing that preserves both source audios

results.metric: Performance on AudioCaps test set (objective and subjective metrics)
results.value: TANGO outperforms AudioLDM on most metrics and is comparable on the rest, despite using 63x less training data

novelty_claims: First to use a frozen instruction-tuned LLM (FLAN-T5) as the text encoder for text-to-audio generation, instead of a joint text-audio encoder like CLAP or a non-instruction-tuned model.
Demonstrates that LDM can learn text-to-audio mapping from a much smaller dataset when guided by a strong instruction-tuned LLM, without fine-tuning the text encoder.
Introduces audio pressure level-based mixing for data augmentation, improving over random mixing used in prior works.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Music ControlNet: a diffusion-based music generation model with multiple precise, time-varying controls (melody, dynamics, rhythm)","Adaptation of ControlNet and Uni-ControlNet approaches from image generation to music generation","Extraction of control signals (melody, dynamics, rhythm) from training audio to create paired data","Fine-tuning a diffusion-based conditional generative model over audio spectrograms given control inputs","A new masking strategy to allow partially-specified time-varying controls at inference"],"problems":["Lack of precise, time-varying control in text-to-music generation models","Difficulty of expressing and controlling lower-level musical attributes (notes, dynamics, rhythm) via text","Scarcity of precise, ground-truth control inputs in training data for music generation models"],"datasets":["Extracted control signals from music audio (details not specified in the provided text)","Created control signals simulating those expected from creators (details not specified in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Faithfulness to input melody"],"results":[{"metric":"Faithfulness to input melody","value":"49% more faithful than MusicGen"}],"novelty_claims":["First diffusion-based music generation model to offer multiple, precise, time-varying controls (melody, dynamics, rhythm) in addition to global text-based style control","Adaptation of image-domain ControlNet/Uni-ControlNet composable control strategies to the music domain","Introduction of a new masking strategy for partially-specified time-varying controls, enabling musical improvisation in unspecified regions","Demonstration of generalization from extracted controls seen during training to creator-expected controls","Achieves higher faithfulness to melody input than MusicGen while using 35x fewer parameters and 11x less training data, and enabling two additional forms of time-varying control"]}

methods: Music ControlNet: a diffusion-based music generation model with multiple precise, time-varying controls (melody, dynamics, rhythm)

Extraction of control signals (melody, dynamics, rhythm) from training audio to create paired data
Fine-tuning a diffusion-based conditional generative model over audio spectrograms given control inputs
A new masking strategy to allow partially-specified time-varying controls at inference
problems: Lack of precise, time-varying control in text-to-music generation models

Created control signals simulating those expected from creators (details not specified in the provided text)

results.value: 49% more faithful than MusicGen

novelty_claims: First diffusion-based music generation model to offer multiple, precise, time-varying controls (melody, dynamics, rhythm) in addition to global text-based style control
Adaptation of image-domain ControlNet/Uni-ControlNet composable control strategies to the music domain
Introduction of a new masking strategy for partially-specified time-varying controls, enabling musical improvisation in unspecified regions
Demonstration of generalization from extracted controls seen during training to creator-expected controls
Achieves higher faithfulness to melody input than MusicGen while using 35x fewer parameters and 11x less training data, and enabling two additional forms of time-varying control
